# construct

> The Construct — an interactive-fiction engine that loads a stored world
> into a played, remembered holonovel. A host over pattern-buffer: it
> narrates scenes, voices characters, and drives a hidden story arc while
> the engine beneath it keeps the world coherent — object permanence,
> per-character knowledge frames, as-of queries. Put a thing down, walk
> away, come back: it's still there. Pre-1.0, first-playable.

Vocabulary: pattern-buffer holds a world's pattern -> the Construct loads
it into a played -> holonovel (one played world, ingested from a book or
built live).

## Docs

- [README.md](README.md): what it is, the live-play acceptance results,
  architecture, how to play. Start here.
- [docs/CONCEPT.md](docs/CONCEPT.md): founding brief — vision, session-zero,
  host architecture, the arc layer, model-provider interface.
- [docs/design/ARC-LAYER.md](docs/design/ARC-LAYER.md): pacing as
  navigation toward a hidden authored destination; anti-railroading by
  construction. The project's design contribution.
- [docs/design/TURN-LOOP.md](docs/design/TURN-LOOP.md): the per-turn DAG
  (serial mutation spine + parallel assembly fan-out) and model tiers.
- [docs/design/SESSION-ZERO.md](docs/design/SESSION-ZERO.md): the unified
  creation interview (ingest a book OR build live) and the
  scenario/playthrough model.
- [docs/design/PROVIDER-INTERFACE.md](docs/design/PROVIDER-INTERFACE.md):
  bring-any-LLM provider boundary; zero-credit subscription default.
- [docs/LEXICON.md](docs/LEXICON.md): naming discipline.

## Built on

- pattern-buffer (https://github.com/5000Stadia/pattern-buffer): the
  append-only world-state engine. The Construct is a host over it; it
  never reimplements engine concepts.
- Kernos (https://github.com/5000Stadia/Kernos): the cohort architecture
  the turn loop borrows.
